criteria.name: Problem Decomposition
criteria.description: Ability to break down the ill-defined problem into specific components related to CPU scheduling.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all relevant components such as process arrival times, burst times, priorities, and CPU scheduling criteria.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misses key components like process priorities or misidentifies irrelevant details as important.

criteria.name: Terminology Usage
criteria.description: Correct use of CPU scheduling terminology to define the problem.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Uses terms such as 'preemptive', 'non-preemptive', 'context switch', and 'throughput' correctly.
criteria.poor_indicator: Confuses terms like 'preemptive' and 'non-preemptive' or uses incorrect terminology.

criteria.name: Goal Specification
criteria.description: Clearly defines the goal of the CPU scheduling task.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Specifies goals such as minimizing turnaround time or maximizing CPU utilization.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to specify a clear goal or sets conflicting objectives.

typical_errors: Ignoring process priorities in problem framing.
Setting unclear or conflicting scheduling goals.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Ability to identify errors in AI-generated CPU scheduling solutions.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly identifies all errors such as incorrect waiting time calculations or improper context switch handling.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to notice significant errors or incorrectly identifies correct outputs as errors.

criteria.name: Performance Metric Evaluation
criteria.description: Evaluates AI solutions based on CPU scheduling performance metrics.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately assesses metrics like average waiting time and CPU utilization.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misinterprets performance metrics or focuses on irrelevant metrics.

criteria.name: Algorithm Suitability Assessment
criteria.description: Judges the suitability of the scheduling algorithm used by AI for the given task.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly assesses whether the chosen algorithm (e.g., Round Robin, SJF) is appropriate for the task requirements.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to recognize algorithm limitations or suggests inappropriate algorithms.

typical_errors: Overlooking context switch penalties in performance evaluation.
Misjudging the appropriateness of a scheduling algorithm.
Failing to differentiate between similar scheduling metrics.

criteria.name: Correction Command Precision
criteria.description: Clarity and precision of commands given to AI to correct scheduling errors.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides clear, precise commands that directly address identified errors, using correct scheduling terminology.
criteria.poor_indicator: Commands are vague, incorrect, or do not address the identified errors.

criteria.name: Algorithm Adjustment
criteria.description: Effectiveness in steering AI towards using a more suitable scheduling algorithm.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Successfully guides AI to switch to a more appropriate algorithm based on task requirements.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to suggest an appropriate algorithm change or suggests unsuitable alternatives.

criteria.name: Performance Criteria Adjustment
criteria.description: Ability to guide AI in refining performance criteria for better scheduling outcomes.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Successfully refines criteria like reducing waiting time or increasing throughput as per task goals.
criteria.poor_indicator: Suggests irrelevant criteria adjustments or does not align with task goals.

typical_errors: Giving imprecise or incorrect correction commands.
Suggesting inappropriate algorithm changes without considering task requirements.
Focusing on irrelevant performance criteria adjustments.
